channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Rubio in Israel says Hamas must be eradicated, casting further doubt on Gaza's shaky ceasefire
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio on Sunday fully endorsed Israel's war aims in the Gaza Strip, saying Hamas “must be eradicated” and throwing the shaky ceasefire into further doubt.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE and NATALIE MELZER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Food delivery driver helped stop suspect following deadly stabbing: officials
item.description: A 23-year-old with possible connections to the Islamic State group is in custody after a stabbing of six passersby that left a 14-year-old boy dead.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Pope is stable, followed Mass on television as he recovers from respiratory infection, Vatican says
item.description: The Vatican says Pope Francis is stable and that he followed Mass on television after a second night sleeping well at the hospital.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Winfield, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Gold mine collapse kills 42 in Mali
item.description: Late on Saturday, Malian television announced the collapse of the site at Bilali Koto in the commune of Dabia, with a provisional death toll of 42 and many injured.
item.dc:creator: BABA AHMED, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Finland president urges Europe to firm up Ukraine's hand for US-sought talks with Russia to end war
item.description: Finland’s president has urged the rearming of Ukraine and putting “maximum pressure on Russia” in the lead-up to possible peace negotiations sought by the U.S. The comments by Finnish President Alexander Stubb came on the final day of the Munich Security Conference on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Emma Burrows, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Rwanda-backed rebels reach the center of east Congo’s 2nd major city in an unprecedented expansion
item.description: Rwanda-backed rebels have reached the center of east Congo’s second-largest city, Bukavu and are present in most parts of the city after little resistance from government forces.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: At least 18 people dead after stampede at New Delhi railway station
item.description: “The crowd went out of control and no one could control it,” said Nikhil Kumar, a shopkeeper who witnessed the crowd surge.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US presented Ukraine with a document to access its minerals but offered almost nothing in return
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy says he directed his ministers not to sign off on a proposed agreement to give the United States access to Ukraine’s rare earth minerals because the document was too focused on U.S. interests.
item.dc:creator: Emma Burrows, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Students in Iran continue protests over 19-year-old’s murder on campus for second day
item.description: Student protesters in Iran called out officials at the University of Tehran for a second day on Saturday following the murder of a business student on campus.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A 23-year-old man stabbed 6 people in Austria killing 1 in what police describe as a random attack
item.description: VIENNA (AP) — A 23-year-old man stabbed six passersby in southern Austria on Saturday in what police said was a random attack that left a 14-year-old dead and five others injured. The suspect was detained in the city of Villach, where the attack took place, police said.
item.dc:creator: STEPHANIE LICHTENSTEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rwanda-backed rebels advance and Congolese forces retreat from eastern Congo's second largest city
item.description: GOMA, Congo (AP) — Panic swept through eastern Congo’s second-largest city on Saturday as residents and soldiers fled by the thousands, scrambling to escape the looming advance of Rwanda-backed rebels.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN KABUMBA, SAM METZ and RODNEY MUHUMUZA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: White South Africans gather in support of Trump and his claims that they are victims of racism
item.description: Some white South Africans have showed support for President Donald Trump and gathered at the U.S. Embassy in Pretoria to claim they are victims of racism by their own government.
item.dc:creator: Alfonso Nqunjana And Gerald Imray, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Shattering the 'iron' ceiling: New series 'Guerrera' explores vital role of US female troops in combat
item.description: A new series released on Amazon, YouTube nd Google TV uncovers the little-known details about the team of women who tasked to special forces during the War on Terror and helped change the course of U.S. history.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Kentucky bourbon bottles signed by the pope raise thousands for charity
item.description: It sounds like the opening line of a joke: What happens when the pope gets his hands on two bottles of Kentucky bourbon?
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER and DYLAN LOVAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: A drone pierced the outer shell of Ukraine's Chernobyl nuclear plant. Radiation levels are normal
item.description: CHERNOBYL NUCLEAR POWER STATION, Ukraine (AP) — A drone armed with a warhead hit the protective outer shell of Ukraine's Chernobyl nuclear plant early Friday, punching a hole in the structure and briefly starting a fire, in an attack Kyiv blamed on Russia. The Kremlin denied it was responsible.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV and EFREM LUKATSKY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Europe quietly works on a plan to send troops to Ukraine for post-war security
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — Increasingly alarmed that U.S. security priorities lie elsewhere, a group of European countries has been quietly working on a plan to send troops into Ukraine to help enforce any future peace settlement with Russia.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: German prosecutors investigate possible Islamic extremist motive for Munich car-ramming attack
item.description: MUNICH (AP) — The suspect in the car-ramming attack in Munich that injured 39 people appears to have had an Islamic extremist motive, but there's no evidence that he was involved with any radical network, authorities said Friday.
item.dc:creator: PHILIPP JENNE and GEIR MOULSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope has respiratory tract infection, is in fair condition and running mild fever, Vatican says
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis was hospitalized Friday after a weeklong bout of bronchitis worsened and was receiving drug therapy for a respiratory tract infection, the Vatican said.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hamas to free another American in Saturday hostage release
item.description: Hamas has released the names of the next three hostages who will be released on Saturday Feb. 15, including American-Israeli Sagui Dekel-Chen, the second American to be released since Trump took office.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Taiwan pledges to communicate and invest more in the US after Trump tariff threats
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — Taiwan President Lai Ching-te said Friday he would communicate more with the U.S. over President Donald Trump’s concerns over the chip industry and invest more in the U.S, hours after Trump ordered reciprocal tariffs on trade partners.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Archaeologists unearth ruins of nearly 2,000-year-old Roman basilica in London
item.description: While exploring the site of a 32-story planned skyscraper in London, archaeologists unearthed the ruins of a nearly 2,000-year-old Roman basilica.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A driver plows into a demonstration in Munich, injuring 30 people
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — A driver drove a car into a labor union demonstration in central Munich on Thursday, injuring 30 people including children, authorities said. Officials said it was believed to be an attack. The suspect, an Afghan asylum-seeker, was arrested.
item.dc:creator: GEIR MOULSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Gas explosion at Taiwan food court kills 4 and injures 26
item.description: A gas explosion at a department store in Taiwan on Thursday killed four people and injured 26, fire authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: O'Reilly predicts Trump and Putin will 'get a ceasefire' in Ukraine
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy "has no power" in opposing a ceasefire deal, according to O'Reilly.
item.dc:creator: Chris Cuomo, NewsNation

item.title: Israel amassing troops in and around Gaza ahead of hostage deadline
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu warned that the Gaza ceasefire will end if Hamas does not release the remaining hostages this upcoming weekend.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Jack the Ripper's identity revealed after DNA match, historian says
item.description: A historian believes he has determined Jack the Ripper's identity through a DNA match of a shawl found at the scene of one of his murders.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: King of Jordan urges patience as Trump presses Gaza takeover
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday dug in on his vision for a U.S. takeover of the Gaza Strip as he sat alongside King Abdullah II of Jordan, who expressed reservations about the idea.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9

item.title: Hamas' threat to delay hostage release raises fears for ceasefire
item.description: The next handover of three hostages had been scheduled for Saturday, and families say time is running out for those still alive.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Video: Rare ‘Black Demon’ fish spotted alive for first time
item.description: The scary-looking fish is usually found in water depths of 1,500 meters, where little to no sunlight reaches.
item.dc:creator: Celeste Houmard, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's AI ambition and China's DeepSeek overshadow an AI summit in Paris
item.description: PARIS (AP) — The geopolitics of artificial intelligence will be in focus at a major summit in France where world leaders, executives and experts will hammer out pledges on guiding the development of the rapidly advancing technology.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET and KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, NewsNation
